title: pluchea phagnaloides
intro: Pluchea phagnaloides, also known as East Indian leadwort, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to tropical Asia and widely cultivated in other parts of the world for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming East Indian leadwort has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can also be used to treat skin conditions and wounds, or as a topical application for relieving pain and inflammation.
